title: Targeted metabolomic profiling of gastric cancer biopsies.
publicationName: The Indian journal of medical research
journalName: The Indian journal of medical research
snippet: Background and objectives Gastric cancer, the fifth most diagnosed malignancy worldwide and seventh in India, exhibits high metabolic activity and marked heterogeneity. In this study, we performed targeted metabolomic profiling of gastric cancer biopsies obtained through esophagogastroduodenoscopy in a South Indian cohort. Methods Targeted metabolite profil...

title: Shrimp Oil Enhances Cognition by Suppressing Neuroinflammation in a Chronic Cerebral Hypoperfusion Rat Model.
snippet: Vascular dementia (VaD) is the second most common age-related condition worldwide in aging populations. Neuroinflammation is a key contributor to the pathological process of VaD. Shrimp oil is a by-product of shrimp processing that is rich in astaxanthin and lipids. This study aimed to investigate the effect of shrimp oil on neuroinflammation and its underl...

title: The RELA-FTH1 axis in senescent cancer-associated fibroblasts creates a stromal iron sink that drives ferroptosis resistance and therapy failure across solid malignant tumors.
publicationName: Drug resistance updates : reviews and commentaries in antimicrobial and anticancer chemotherapy
journalName: Drug resistance updates : reviews and commentaries in antimicrobial and anticancer chemotherapy
snippet: Therapy resistance remains the foremost barrier to improving outcomes in solid malignancies. While tumor-cell-intrinsic resistance mechanisms have been extensively studied, the contribution of the tumor microenvironment (TME) to treatment failure is poorly understood. Cellular senescence accumulates across diverse solid tumor types and reshapes the stromal....

title: Cytogenetic and Molecular Analysis of a "Double-Hit" RUNX1 Including a RUNX1 p.Trp279* and a Cryptic Novel t(6;21)(q25;q22)/RUNX1::ARID1B in Acute Myeloid Leukemia.
snippet: Alterations involving RUNX1 are recurrent in hematologic malignancies and contribute to disease pathogenesis via dysregulation of transcriptional factors essential for hematopoiesis. Here, we report an acquired alteration in both alleles of RUNX1; one is a truncating mutation and the second is a novel RUNX1::ARID1B identified in acute myeloid leukemia. Bone...
